item.title: Gibson Les Paul Jr DC Tribute Bass — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Very Good · Castle of Tone

Selling this black LP Jr. These highly regarded, discontinued, USA-made short scale basses are *awesome* but I have two of them and reach for my modded one more. That one is genuinely my main player these days - these have some of the best ergonomics and necks I have ever felt. The hype is justified!

Original gig bag (the big triangular one) is included in the sale. Please see photos, the back has some light buckle rash and a small "matte" looking spot, I think it must've been a rubber strap or something that interacted with the finish. It's just a small spot and could probably be polished, but it never bothered me enough to go down that road. Overall, the bass is in excellent condition and has only seen very light use.

Please message with any questions and thanks for looking!

item.title: Fender American Standard Jazz Bass 2004 - 3 Tone Sunburst — $1,446.29
item.description:
$1,446.29 · Excellent · Hillsound

Fender American Standard Jazz Bass 2004 3 Tone Sunburst Modified Pre Owned

Commission Sale.

This Fender Jazz is in excellent shape for it's 20+ years. It only has a few dinks, some of which have been touched up, mainly on the back or side.

There are some sensible upgrades / modification ie it has Seymour Duncan Basslines branded pickups and a Badass II bridge. Original parts are not supplied but the original Fender hard case is and the instrument is currently strung with Thomastik Flatwounds.

There is also a push push boost on the main volume knob. All things considered this is an excellent value Fender American Jazz, so highly recommended.

item.title: Custom Telecaster(ish) Bass Guitar 2025 - Walnut/Maple — $875
item.description:
$875 · Excellent · g's Gear Locker

I made the body; the neck is an Allparts TBRO Fender-licensed Telecaster Bass Neck, maple with rosewood fretboard.

Gotoh GB640 RES-O-LITE lightweight tuning machines.

Fender string retainer.

The body is semi-hollow, made from maple, and the top is 1/4" black walnut, with black checkerboard binding. I've included a few photos of the build.

Fender Vintage-style Bass Bridge.

The neck pickup is a high-output Artec EBC4 Ceramic Sidewinder Mudbucker Style Bass Pickup, measures 31.4K resistance on my multimeter.

The bridge pickup is a mini humbucker from True Custom Shop, measures 9.8K resistance.

Fender Concentric 250K/500K Solid Shaft Volume and Tone Pots, with Fender 60s-style Concentric Knobs.

Rickenbacker-type pickup selector switch.

This bass is wired like a Rickenbacker 4001/4003 equipped with Rick-O-Sound (wiring diagram that I followed is in photos). In case you're not familiar, there are two output jacks, one labeled STANDARD and, on this bass, the other labeled STEREO-SPLIT; the STANDARD jack provides normal neck/bridge/both function with the selector switch, while the STEREO-SPLIT allows one to (with a stereo to mono splitter, which I'll include) send the neck pickup signal to one amp, and the bridge pickup signal to another. There are lots of YouTube videos available if you'd like a more in-depth explanation.

This has been a working bass and has the attendant scratches, dings, scars, etc, but is in overall excellent condition and looks, plays, and sounds great.

item.title: Warwick RB Star Bass 5 Creme White 2013 Semi-Hollow Electric Bass — $1,027.16
item.description:
$1,027.16 · Very Good · Sell Us Your Guitar

Here for sale we have a Warwick RB Star Bass 5 Semi-Hollow Electric Bass in Creme White finish. Made in China in 2013, this bass consists of a semi-hollow Maple body, a Maple neck, and a 21-fret Wenge fingerboard. Other appointments include Warwick machine heads, Chrome hardware, a Warwick bridge and tailpiece, and two Passive MEC Vintage Single Coil pickups. These pickups are wired to two volume controls, two tone controls, and a three-way pickup selector.


The Maple neck plays superbly well, with the Warwick neck profile feeling somewhat like a 'D' shape. The profile is fairly substantial, but with the slightly flattened area to the centre rear encouraging ergonomic thumb positioning, it's easy to reach notes across the register. Paired with generous double-cutaway body design and set-neck construction, the full range of the instrument is easily accessible. The Wenge of the fingerboard provides a tactile yet durable playing surface.  


The Warwick sound is well known for its deep growl, and the tonewood selection helping to secure a reputation amongst players for a versatile yet distinct tone that will stand out from the crowd. There are of course plenty of deep, rumbling lows which will fill out the band mix, whilst the punchy mids and sparkling top end help to create a driving sound and sense of urgency by capturing the attack of your playing, whilst maintaining a vintage vibe that is sure to inspire the player. The on-board controls offer comprehensive tonal opportunities whilst maintaining a simple and non-intrusive character on the playing experience. 


Brand- Warwick

Model- RB Star Bass 5

Finish- Creme White

Year- 2013

Made In- China

Body- Maple

Neck- Maple

Neck Profile- 'D'

Fingerboard- Wenge

Fingerboard Radius- 26"

Frets- 21

Scale Length- 32"

Nut Width- 1.75"

Aesthetic Condition:

Headstock: Overall excellent condition. Light finish imperfection can be seen to the front face top edge, this is from the factory. Light to mild scratching is present to the front face, the most apparent of these can be seen around the E tuning machine.

Neck: Overall very good condition. Moderate scratching and marking is present to the rear of the neck heel, the most apparent of these has exposed the wood beneath. A light scratching is present to the 3rd fret of the fingerboard, this can not be felt in the playing position.  

Body: Overall very good condition. Mild scratching is present to the the center of the rear face, these have exposed the wood beneath consistent with past use. Light discoloration is present to the bass side lower bout edge, this can be seen under close scrutiny. A light indentation is present to the treble side lower bout edge, this has lightly discolored the finish.


Condition of Frets:

The frets demonstrate light signs of use, not affecting playability.  There is moderate material remaining for any fret work the new owner may wish to perform.


Current Set Up:

Truss Rod: Fully functional. Truss rod tool (5mm hex) not included.

Hardware: Overall very good condition. All aspects of the metal hardware display light scratching and tarnishing of the finish, this is most apparent to the pickup covers. The A tuning machine has been replaced with an official Warwick tuning machine.

Structural: No concerns or issues to note.

item.title: Ibanez ATK 300 Bass (1994 *First Year of Production) Natural Ash / Maple Board — $395
item.description:
$395 · Very Good · Joseph's Boutique

Up for sale is a highly sought-after, first-year production 1994 Ibanez ATK 300 active bass in Very Good condition. Built at the legendary Cort factory in Korea (Serial: C444415), this era of the ATK is famous for its incredible build quality and its ability to deliver massive, punchy tones that rival a Music Man StingRay.

The Specs

  • Year / Origin: 1994, Made in Korea (Cort Factory)
  • Body: Solid Ash
  • Neck / Fingerboard: Maple neck with 22-fret maple fingerboard (black dot inlays)
  • Pickup: Original ATK-A Triple-Coil humbucker
  • Electronics: Active 3-band EQ (9V powered) with a 3-way character switch (Traditional, Bright, and Attack modes)
  • Bridge: Heavy-duty ATK massive bridge (allows through-body or through-bridge stringing)

Condition Details
This bass is in Very Good cosmetic and structural condition, especially considering it is over 30 years old!

  • Electronics: 100% functional. Pots and the 3-way selector switch are quiet and crackle-free. The active EQ provides massive tonal shaping.
  • Neck & Frets: The maple neck is straight, the truss rod adjusts perfectly, and the frets show minimal wear with plenty of life left. Low, comfortable action.
  • Cosmetics: Light clear-coat surface swirling and minor superficial playing wear as expected for its age. No major dings, cracks, or gouges through the finish.
  • Hardware: The original massive bridge and tuners hold stability perfectly. 
  • Note - battery cover not missing like many are.

Why the '94 Korean Models?
***Players actively hunt down these early Korean models because the triple-coil pickup and heavy ash body create an incredibly aggressive, mid-range punch that easily cuts through any live mix or recording project. It is an absolute workhorse.***

Shipping & Accessories

  • Ships securely packed in a Custom Fit Hardshell Case (Pictured)
  • Will be detuned slightly and double-boxed for safe transit.

Please feel free to reach out with any questions or if you need specific photos! Thanks for looking.

item.title: Fender American Reissue 1962 Jazz Bass 2007 - Olympic White — $1,500
item.description:
$1,500 · Very Good · Mike's Gear Bazaar

Here's a nearly twenty-year old American Vintage Jazz Bass that's on the light-ish side, has great resonance and responsiveness and sounds killer when plugged in. It's wrapped in this bassist's personal favorite Fender color; Olympic White with just a bit of yellowing from age. This model features the early 1960s configuration, including the concentric "stack-knob" volume/tone controls, dual vintage-spec single-coil pickups, and reverse-gear tuners. This one is just begging to get back into action in the studio and/or on stage and will deliver the J-Bass goods every time! Ships in hardshell case.

Year: 2007

Origin: Corona, CA, USA

Color: Olympic White

Weight: 8lbs 14oz

Neck details/fret details: 4-bolt medium profile, thin to medium Maple neck with Rosewood fretboard.

Modifications/Repairs: pickguard has been changed to white

Case/Extras: Comes with non-original hard case that fits the bass well. Case is in very good shape.

Cosmetic Condition: Good condition with some light dings, marks in some spots, marks, and light checking. See pics.

Operational Condition: Fully-functioning including truss rod, pickups, electronics, etc.

item.title: Ken Smith 1997 BSR 4J - Natural — $3,999.99
item.description:
$3,999.99 · Very Good · AK Guitars

Beautiful Ken Smith J! Awesome figuring on 3 piece maple body. Plays excellently with low action and good set up on thin 3 piece maple neck. Nice rosewood board with medium jumbo frets and very little fretwear. The original Smith active electronics sound killer! Bright and articulate with strong mid-low punch and clear highs. The bass is very clean for its age with just two players spots on the front. The deeper of the two in between the pickups from thumb rest and another wear area above the neck pickup not as noticeable. The back is very clean with a few tiny dings around the control cavity area but no scratches or belt rash. Overall looks great and well kept although played thoroughly! Awesome player with great tone and cool look. Good weight too!


Comes with the original hard shell case.

8.23 lbs

Used Spector Performer 5 5-String Bass Guitar Metallic Blue


The Spector Performer 5 is a comfortable, great-playing 5-string bass that delivers the signature Spector feel and modern styling at an affordable price. Finished in a striking Metallic Blue gloss finish, this bass features the iconic carved body shape, slim maple neck, and a rosewood fingerboard that makes it easy to play while providing excellent balance and comfort.


Loaded with dual soapbar pickups and active electronics, the Performer 5 offers a wide range of tones suitable for rock, metal, worship, country, and modern pop. The 5-string configuration provides an extended low B for added versatility, while the black hardware complements the bold finish for a sleek appearance.


Features:


• Metallic Blue gloss finish

• 5-string bass

• Contoured solid body

• Maple neck

• Rosewood fingerboard

• 24 frets

• Dual soapbar pickups

• Active electronics

• Master Volume

• Pickup Blend

• Bass Boost/Cut

• Treble Boost/Cut

• Black hardware

• Fixed 5-string bridge

• Crafted in Indonesia

• Serial Number: WI20090633


Condition:


This bass is in very good overall condition and has been tested and is fully functional. It shows light cosmetic wear from normal use with a few minor surface scratches and small handling marks, but no significant chips or structural damage were found. The finish remains glossy and vibrant, the neck is clean, and the hardware is in very good condition. Frets show minimal wear with plenty of life remaining. Overall, this is a clean, great-playing Spector that is ready for the stage or studio.

item.title: Maruszczyk Elwood 4a Gloss White Electric Bass — $1,737.20
item.description:
$1,737.20 · Excellent · Sell Us Your Guitar

Here for sale we have a Maruszczyk Elwood 4a Electric Bass in White Gloss finish. Made in Poland, this bass consists of an Alder body, a Maple neck, and a 21-fret Painted Gloss fingerboard. Other appointments include Gold Hardware, Hipshot Vintage-Style tuners, a top-loading 4-saddle bridge, Gold frets, and a set of EMG X Active Single Coil pickups. These pickups are wired to a master volume, pickup balancer, and a master tone. 


The Maple neck sits comfortably in the hand, with the shape feeling like a  ‘D’ profile allowing for comfortable reach in all registers and promoting ergonomic thumb positioning, whilst the Gloss finish is fantastic for smooth, quick transitions up and down the neck. The Gloss Painted fingerboard feels pleasant under the fingers, whilst the 14” radius and Medium Jumbo frets allow for ease of different techniques such as slap bass or chordal playing. The sleek body design allows the player to have unobstructed access to the entire register, whilst also making longer durations of playing a comfortable experience.


The EMG X Active Single Coil pickups are bright and articulate and deliver a high output. Each pickup reacts with the strings producing different tonalities on thicker or thinner strings. The thicker strings provide a more thunderous tone with subtle distorted frequencies, whereas the thinner strings produce a crisp tone with more dynamic response. This bass is capable of a wide range of tones thanks to the controls that are featured. The Blend feature allows for both pickups to work together to produce a punchier, thicker sound.

Model- Elwood 4a

Made In- Poland

Used Squier Classic Vibe '70s Jazz Bass Natural Maple Fingerboard


This Squier Classic Vibe '70s Jazz Bass delivers vintage-inspired Fender styling with modern reliability and excellent playability. Finished in a beautiful Natural gloss finish that highlights the wood grain, this bass features a maple neck with a bound maple fingerboard and black block inlays for the unmistakable look of a classic '70s Jazz Bass.


Equipped with a pair of Fender-designed alnico single-coil Jazz Bass pickups, this instrument offers the articulate growl, punch, and versatility that has made the Jazz Bass a staple across nearly every style of music. The slim neck profile and comfortable offset body make it equally at home on stage, in the studio, or in the practice room.


Features:


• Natural gloss finish

• Poplar body

• Maple neck

• Bound maple fingerboard with black block inlays

• 20 narrow tall frets

• Fender-designed Alnico single-coil Jazz Bass pickups

• Individual volume controls for each pickup

• Master tone control

• 4-saddle vintage-style bridge

• Open-gear vintage-style tuning machines

• 34" scale length

• Crafted in Indonesia (2020)


Condition:


This bass is in very good overall condition and has been tested and plays great. It shows light to moderate cosmetic wear from normal use, including a few small dings and finish impressions on the body. The most noticeable blemishes include a small ding on the back of the body and a few minor marks around the edges, all of which are shown in the photos. The gloss finish still presents very well, the hardware is clean, and the frets show minimal wear with plenty of life remaining. Overall, this is a clean, great-playing Classic Vibe Jazz Bass ready for its next owner.

item.title: Atelier Z m245 jb77 Ash — $2,300
item.description:
$2,300 · Excellent · The Purple Couch Llc

If you're chasing that Marcus Miller-style punch and snap with the smooth, effortless feel of a boutique Jazz bass, this 2025 Atelier Z JB-77 is exactly that. To me, it has the aggressive, hi-fi tone associated with Marcus Miller while feeling incredibly refined and comfortable to play—very much in the same vein as a high-end Sadowsky.

The bass is absolutely mint and has only been played out a handful of times. It has spent the rest of its life in my smoke-free home. There are no dings, scratches, or issues of any kind.

The only reason I'm selling it is simple—I just don't find myself reaching for 4-string basses anymore, so this incredible bass deserves to be played.

  • 2025 Atelier Z JB-77
  • 4-String
  • Made in Japan
  • Active Electronics
  • Incredible slap tone with tight lows, punchy mids, and crystal-clear highs
  • Fast, comfortable neck
  • Boutique Japanese craftsmanship
  • Mint Condition

Includes:

If you've been looking for an Atelier Z without the wait or import hassle, this is an outstanding example that's ready for the stage or studio.

This is a serious instrument for bassists who need authentic acoustic character without sacrificing amplification versatility.

Build & Hardware

item.title: Warwick RockBass Streamer LX 4 — $655.18
item.description:
$655.18 · Excellent · JSMusic

Hello,

I'm selling this Warwick RockBass Streamer LX 4 in a red metallic finish, including a Harley

Benton light case.


I bought this bass in December 2025 and have played it for less than ten hours since then (I’m actually a guitarist and rarely use a bass, except to record individual tracks for a project). A few weeks ago, the electronics were checked and repaired at the Thomann Service Center (the bridge pickup wasn’t producing any output). They also smoothed out the sharp fret ends there. Fixing the battery in its place is a bit of a challenge. Even in its stock configuration, there’s barely enough space in the electronics compartment, and until now, the battery clamp couldn’t be screwed in securely enough. That’s why the technician implemented a solution using foam padding; this way, nothing rattles—see Photo 18.

Just a few days ago, I strung a fresh set of Elixir Nanoweb-coated strings, .045–.105.

By the way, the playability is—especially now, after the frets have been dressed—truly excellent, and the bass sits comfortably against the body.

Photos 3 and 4 were taken outdoors in the evening sun and have not been edited.

There is a small, even hard to see paint flaw near the front pickup; please see Photo 17.

The bass comes with its Harley Benton “light case,” packing material, and outer box. It will be shipped via DHL with a tracking number and is adequately insured.

Here are the specs:

•    Body: Carolina

•    3-strip maple neck with Ekanga veneer strips

•    Fingerboard: Wenge

•    Fingerboard radius: 508 mm

•    Scale length: 864 mm (Long Scale)

•    Nut width: 38.5 mm

•    Just a Nut III Tedur nut

•    24 jumbo nickel-silver frets

•    Pickups: passive MEC “Dynamic Correction” P/J

•    Active RockBass 2-band electronics

•    Controls for volume, balance, bass, and treble

•    2-piece Warwick bridge

•    String spacing: 19 mm

•    Warwick tuners

•    Warwick Security Locks

•    Chrome hardware

•    Color: High-gloss Metallic Red

This sale is made without any warranty or liability for material defects, as it is a private sale!

Since the EU DIRECTIVE provides for a 1-year warranty even for private sales—unless the seller expressly excludes it—I hereby declare that I do not provide any warranty for the items I sell privately within the meaning of the EU Directive. By purchasing the goods, you expressly agree to waive the warranty. There is no right of withdrawal or return for used items purchased from me (§312d(4)(5) BGB).

I ship within the EU.

Feel free to message me.

Best regards,

Juergen

A 3-piece maple neck with consistent dimensions—0.787" at the 1st fret and 0.866" at the 12th—delivers stability and comfortable ergonomics, while the Graph Tech XL Black Tusq 45mm nut ensures reliable tuning and clean string-to-fret contact. A Schecter USA M-25 5-string bridge pickup and Schecter USA J-25 5-string neck pickup deliver articulate, defined tones across the frequency spectrum. Dual mini-toggle switches provide series/split/parallel voicing options on the bridge pickup and coil-tap functionality on the neck pickup, allowing players to shape everything from tight, punchy lows to warm, singing highs without leaving the instrument.

item.title: 1964 Hofner 500/1 — $3,846.66
item.description:
$3,846.66 · Very Good · Vintage Rare & Retro Guitars

The 500/1, which had been produced by Hofner for only European consumption since 1956, was finally introduced into the UK by Selmer in 1963 as the Violin Bass - Used, then and now, by a gentleman called Sir Paul McCartney.

This 1964 Hofner 500/1 Bass is all original, although the bridge pickup has been rewound. Pot codes are for 1963 which is common for a ’64 Hofner bass. The bass is in super condition considering its 60 years of age – although, as you would expect, it does have some very small marks and dings on the body (see last 6 pictures), and the edge has broken of the treble side of the string guide above the zero fret. The back of the neck has a slightly darker finish than the normal Hofner factory finish - we assume this has been over finished at some point in the past - looking at the whole of the bass under black light this finish was done very early in the bass's life.

The bass comes complete with a modern Hofner Deluxe Tweed finished case which is also in excellent condition.

Shipping within mainland UK using fully insured 24-hour courier service @ £35.00.

Shipping to EU is £85.00 - Shipping to US & Canada is £200.00

Customers outside of UK/EU/US/Canada should contact Vintage Rare & Retro Guitars for a shipping quotation. Free pick-up is always available at our Gullane shop.
